sources.label: Yahoo Finance quote/news 8147.TWO (no fresh headlines since Jun 6; AGM Jun 10)
cnyes intraday alert - 8147 limit-up at 155.5 (dated Apr 10, 2026, not new)
UDN investor-conference recap - 2026 revenue +30% target on AI, humanoid robot, medical (late-2025, pre-existing thesis evidence)
CommonWealth Magazine - Nextronics in NVIDIA, liquid-cooling, optical supply chains

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and a quiet +0.67% tape; yesterday's hold already priced the +40% YTD run against the still-intact micro-cap humanoid-connector thesis, and nothing today changes that setup. May monthly revenue (due ~Jun 10) and the Jun 10 AGM are pending catalysts, not new evidence.
verdicts.bull: Confirmed humanoid trial production (~10 high-value connectors plus ~140 connecting parts per industrial humanoid for BMW/Benz-class lines) on a $190M cap with 2026 revenue guided +30% - genuine bottleneck optionality the market barely covers.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 2026 (EPS NT$0.91, revenue +26% YoY) and April revenue +19.5% YoY keep the growth thesis on track, but robotics is still <1% of revenue, so the humanoid story remains mostly option value while AI server/liquid-cooling connectors carry the P&L.
verdicts.bear: +40% YTD on a thin, retail-heavy TPEx micro-cap with no new wins since the December investor conference means much of the easy rerating is done; any soft May print or AGM disappointment hits an illiquid name hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May 2026 monthly revenue release (~Jun 10) and the June 10 AGM confirming humanoid connector ramp into 2H26 volume orders.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Humanoid revenue stays sub-1% of sales longer than hoped while the YTD rerating deflates; micro-cap illiquidity amplifies any downside.
